title: UFOs, Synchronicities & Prophetic Dreams (Ft. Eric Wargo)
summary: Eric Wargo discusses his theory that precognition is a form of informational time travel, where prophecies are always self-fulfilling because the future influences the present through dreams and symbolic messages. He explores how the brain may function as a hybrid quantum-classical computer, enabling precognitive abilities, and provides numerous examples from art, literature, and scientific research to support his hypothesis that remote viewing and other psychic phenomena are actually forms of precognition.

acronyms.definition: The supposed ability of the mind to influence physical matter or random event generators
Perception occurring independently of known sensory processes, including telepathy and precognition
Brain imaging technique that measures brain activity by detecting changes in blood flow
Research institute that conducted early remote viewing research
U.S. intelligence agency that ran psychic spy programs
U.S. intelligence agency involved in remote viewing research programs

programs_mentioned: Lower Manhattan Cultural Council artist residency

events.description: Frederick Myers introduced the telepathy model at the Society for Psychical Research
Freud had the famous dream that he spent 14 pages interpreting in his book on dreams, which later proved to be precognitive of his oral cancer
Stargate remote viewing program was declassified and supposedly ended
Michael Rolando Richards created his sculpture depicting himself impaled by airplanes
Michael Rolando Richards was killed in the Twin Towers on September 11th, having stayed overnight in his studio

key_claims.claim: Prophecies must be self-fulfilling because events in the future are fixed in the same way events in the past are fixed
Physicists in the 1980s did the math on wormholes and found that paradox doesn't occur because the universe is self-consistent
The brain is a hybrid of classical and quantum systems, not simply a quantum computer
Microtubules have been shown to superconduct, suggesting quantum relationships are occurring within neurons
Remote viewing is actually precognition - remote viewers are seeing future feedback about targets rather than perceiving across space
Joseph McMoneagle won the Legion of Merit for over 200 instances of helping add intelligence in vital cases
Jessica Utts, former president of the American Statistical Association, found overwhelming statistical evidence for psychic functioning
The caudate nucleus and putamen areas of the brain are more neuronally dense in people who see more UFOs and have more premonitions
Ed May's decision augmentation theory suggests researchers' precognition draws them to start experiments at times when random numbers will generate desired results
